evidence_items.evidence_description: In a multicenter phase II trial of imatinib in metastatic mucosal, acral, or chronically sun-damaged melanoma with KIT alterations, patients with KIT mutations showed a significantly higher response rate compared to those with KIT amplification only. Best overall response rate (BORR) was 54% (7 of 13) in KIT-mutated tumors versus 0% in KIT-amplified only tumors. The overall disease control rate was 77% in KIT-mutated versus 18% in KIT-amplified tumors. The difference was statistically significant.

evidence_items.evidence_description: In a multicenter phase II trial of imatinib in metastatic mucosal, acral, or chronically sun-damaged melanoma, patients with KIT amplification only (without KIT mutation) showed 0% response rate to imatinib, compared to 54% in KIT-mutated tumors. The disease control rate was only 18% in KIT-amplified only tumors versus 77% in KIT-mutated tumors. The study concludes that imatinib can be effective when tumors harbor KIT mutations, but not if KIT is amplified only.

evidence_items.evidence_description: In a phase II trial of imatinib in KIT-altered melanoma, four patients harbored pretreatment NRAS mutations (including NRAS G12C, G12A, Q61K, Q61H). The study concludes that NRAS mutations may be a mechanism of therapeutic resistance to imatinib in melanoma.
